   132→def escape_string(s: str) -> str:
   133→    """Process escape sequences in strings"""
   134→    result = []
   135→    i = 0
   136→    while i < len(s):
   137→        if s[i] == '\\' and i + 1 < len(s):
   138→            next_char = s[i+1]
   139→            if next_char == 'n':
   140→                result.append('\n')
   141→                i += 2
   142→            elif next_char == 't':
   143→                result.append('\t')
   144→                i += 2
   145→            elif next_char == 'r':
   146→                result.append('\r')
   147→                i += 2
   148→            elif next_char == '%':
   149→                result.append('%')
   150→                i += 2
   151→            elif next_char == '\\':
   152→                result.append('\\')
   153→                i += 2
   154→            else:
   155→                result.append(s[i])
   156→                i += 1
   157→        else:
   158→            result.append(s[i])
   159→            i += 1
   160→    return ''.join(result)

   174→class Lexer:
   175→    TOKEN_SPECS = [
   176→        ('WHITESPACE', r'[ \t]+'),
   177→        ('NEWLINE', r'\n'),
   178→        ('COMMENT', r'//[^\n]*'),
   179→        ('TASK', r'task'),
   180→        ('PARAMS', r'params:'),

   184→        ('OUTPUT', r'output:'),
   185→        ('TIMEOUT', r'timeout:'),
   186→        ('CACHE', r'cache:'),
   187→        ('CACHED_TASK', r'CachedTask'),
   188→        ('INPUTS', r'inputs:'),
   189→        ('LBRACE', r'\{'),
   190→        ('RBRACE', r'\}'),
   191→        ('LBRACKET', r'\['),
   192→        ('RBRACKET', r'\]'),
   193→        ('LPAREN', r'\('),
   194→        ('RPAREN', r'\)'),
   195→        ('SEMICOLON', r';'),
   196→        ('COMMA', r','),
   197→        ('COLON', r':'),
   198→        ('EQ', r'='),
   199→        ('PLUS_EQ', r'\+=?'),
   200→        ('STRING', r'"[^"]*"|\'[^\']*\''),
   201→        ('NUMBER', r'\d+\.\d+|\d+'),
   202→        ('BOOL', r'TRUE|FALSE'),
   203→        ('ID', r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*'),
   204→        ('DOT', r'\.'),
   205→        ('DOLLAR', r'\$'),
   206→        ('PERCENT', r'%'),
   207→        ('NOT', r'!'),
   208→        ('AND', r'&&'),
   209→        ('OR', r'\|\|'),
   210→        ('LT', r'<'),
   211→        ('GT', r'>'),
   212→        ('LTE', r'<='),
   213→        ('GTE', r'>='),
   214→        ('EQ_EXPR', r'=='),
   215→        ('NEQ', r'!='),
   216→        ('FOR', r'for'),
   217→        ('WHILE', r'while'),
   218→        ('IF', r'if'),
   219→        ('ELSE', r'else'),
   220→        ('ELIF', r'elif'),
   221→        ('RETURN', r'return'),
   222→        ('CONTINUE', r'continue'),
   223→        ('BREAK', r'break'),
   224→        ('EXISTS', r'exists'),

   226→        ('LEN', r'len'),

   231→    def __init__(self, text: str):
   232→        self.text = text
   233→        self.pos = 0
   234→        self.line = 1
   235→        self.col = 1
   236→        self.tokens: list[Token] = []
   237→
   238→    def tokenize(self) -> list[Token]:
   239→        # Build combined regex
   240→        combined = '|'.join(f'(?P<{name}>{pattern})' for name, pattern in self.TOKEN_SPECS)
   241→        regex = re.compile(combined)
   242→
   243→        while self.pos < len(self.text):
   244→            match = regex.match(self.text, self.pos)
   245→            if match:
   246→                token_type = match.lastgroup
   247→                value = match.group()
   248→
   249→                if token_type == 'WHITESPACE':
   250→                    pass
   251→                elif token_type == 'NEWLINE':
   252→                    self.line += 1
   253→                    self.col = 1
   254→                elif token_type == 'COMMENT':
   255→                    pass
   256→                else:
   257→                    self.tokens.append(Token(token_type, value, self.line, self.col))
   258→
   259→                self.pos = match.end()
   260→                self.col = match.end() - (self.text.rfind('\n', 0, self.pos) or -1)
   261→                if self.col < 0:
   262→                    self.col = 0
   263→            else:
   264→                char = self.text[self.pos]
   265→                raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected character '{char}' at line {self.line}, col {self.col}")
   266→
   267→        return self.tokens

   372→class Parser:
   373→    def __init__(self, tokens: list[Token]):
   374→        self.tokens = tokens
   375→        self.pos = 0
   376→        self.length = len(tokens)
   377→
   378→    def peek(self, offset: int = 0) -> Optional[Token]:
   379→        idx = self.pos + offset
   380→        if idx < self.length:
   381→            return self.tokens[idx]
   382→        return None
   383→
   384→    def consume(self, type_: Optional[str] = None, value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   385→        token = self.current()
   386→        if token is None:
   387→            raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected end of input, expected {type_}")
   388→        if type_ is not None and token.type != type_:
   389→            raise SyntaxError(f"Expected {type_}, got {token.type} at line {token.line}, col {token.col}")
   390→        if value is not None and token.value != value:
   391→            raise SyntaxError(f"Expected {value}, got {token.value} at line {token.line}, col {token.col}")
   392→        self.pos += 1
   393→        return token
   394→
   395→    def current(self) -> Optional[Token]:
   396→        if self.pos < self.length:
   397→            return self.tokens[self.pos]
   398→        return None
   399→
   400→    def match(self, type_: str, value: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
   401→        token = self.current()
   402→        if token is None:
   403→            return False
   404→        if value is not None:
   405→            return token.type == type_ and token.value == value
   406→        return token.type == type_
   407→
   408→    def parse_task(self) -> Task:
   409→        self.consume('TASK')
   410→        name_token = self.consume('ID')
   411→        task_name = name_token.value
   412→
   413→        comment = None
   414→        if self.match('COMMENT'):
   415→            comment = self.current().value[2:].strip()
   416→            self.consume('COMMENT')
   417→
   418→        self.consume('LBRACE')
   419→        params = []
   420→        run = None
   421→        success = None
   422→        requires = None
   423→        output = None
   424→        timeout = None
   425→        cache = None
   426→        inputs = None
   427→
   428→        while not self.match('RBRACE'):
   429→            if self.match('PARAMS'):
   430→                params = self.parse_params()
   431→            elif self.match('RUN'):
   432→                self.consume('RUN')
   433→                run = self.parse_block()
   434→            elif self.match('SUCCESS'):
   435→                self.consume('SUCCESS')
   436→                success = self.parse_block_text()
   437→            elif self.match('REQUIRES'):
   438→                self.consume('REQUIRES')
   439→                requires = self.parse_block_text()
   440→            elif self.match('OUTPUT'):
   441→                self.consume('OUTPUT')
   442→                output = self.parse_expression()
   443→                if self.match('SEMICOLON'):
   444→                    self.consume('SEMICOLON')
   445→            elif self.match('TIMEOUT'):
   446→                self.consume('TIMEOUT')
   447→                timeout_expr = self.parse_expression()
   448→                if isinstance(timeout_expr, Literal):
   449→                    timeout = float(timeout_expr.value)
   450→                else:
   451→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Timeout must be a literal number, got {timeout_expr}")
   452→                if self.match('SEMICOLON'):
   453→                    self.consume('SEMICOLON')
   454→            elif self.match('CACHE'):
   455→                self.consume('CACHE')
   456→                cache = self.parse_cache_config()
   457→            elif self.match('INPUTS'):
   458→                self.consume('INPUTS')
   459→                inputs = self.parse_inputs_list()
   460→            else:
   461→                if self.match('NEWLINE'):
   462→                    self.consume('NEWLINE')
   463→                    continue
   464→                raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected token {self.current()} in task definition")
   465→
   466→        self.consume('RBRACE')
   467→        return Task(name=task_name, params=params, run=run, success=success,
   468→                   requires=requires, output=output, timeout=timeout, comment=comment, cache=cache,
   469→                   inputs=inputs)
   470→
   471→    def parse_params(self) -> list[Parameter]:
   472→        self.consume('COLON')
   473→        self.consume('LBRACE')
   474→        params = []
   475→        has_default_param = False
   476→
   477→        while not self.match('RBRACE'):
   478→            if self.match('NEWLINE'):
   479→                self.consume('NEWLINE')
   480→                continue
   481→            if self.match('WHITESPACE'):
   482→                self.consume('WHITESPACE')
   483→                continue
   484→
   485→            name_token = self.consume('ID')
   486→            name = name_token.value
   487→
   488→            type_token = self.consume('ID')
   489→            type_name = type_token.value
   490→
   491→            if type_name not in ('string', 'int', 'float', 'bool', 'list'):
   492→                raise SyntaxError(f"Invalid type {type_name} at line {type_token.line}")
   493→
   494→            default = None
   495→            has_default = False
   496→
   497→            if self.match('EQ'):
   498→                self.consume('EQ')
   499→                has_default = True
   500→                has_default_param = True
   501→                default = self.parse_literal()
   502→
   503→            # Check type validity
   504→            if type_name == 'list':
   505→                if not self.match('LBRACKET'):
   506→                    raise SyntaxError(f"List type requires bracket at line {type_token.line}")
   507→                self.consume('LBRACKET')
   508→                inner_type_token = self.consume('ID')
   509→                inner_type = inner_type_token.value
   510→                if inner_type not in ('string', 'int', 'float', 'bool'):
   511→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Invalid list element type {inner_type} at line {inner_type_token.line}")
   512→                self.consume('RBRACKET')
   513→
   514→            if has_default:
   515→                # Validate default value type
   516→                if not self.validate_default_type(default, type_name):
   517→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Default value {default} doesn't match type {type_name}")
   518→
   519→            if self.match('SEMICOLON'):
   520→                self.consume('SEMICOLON')
   521→
   522→            params.append(Parameter(name=name, type=type_name, default=default, has_default=has_default))
   523→
   524→        self.consume('RBRACE')
   525→        return params
   526→
   527→    def validate_default_type(self, value: Any, type_name: str) -> bool:
   528→        if value is None:
   529→            return True
   530→
   531→        if type_name == 'string':
   532→            return isinstance(value, str)
   533→        elif type_name == 'int':
   534→            return isinstance(value, int)
   535→        elif type_name == 'float':
   536→            return isinstance(value, (int, float))
   537→        elif type_name == 'bool':
   538→            return isinstance(value, bool)
   539→        elif type_name == 'list':
   540→            return isinstance(value, list)
   541→
   542→        return False
   543→
   544→    def parse_literal(self) -> AST:
   545→        token = self.current()
   546→        if token is None:
   547→            raise SyntaxError("Expected literal, got end of input")
   548→
   549→        if token.type == 'STRING':
   550→            self.consume()
   551→            # Remove quotes
   552→            value = escape_string(token.value[1:-1])
   553→            return Literal(value)
   554→        elif token.type == 'NUMBER':
   555→            self.consume()
   556→            if '.' in token.value:
   557→                return Literal(float(token.value))
   558→            else:
   559→                return Literal(int(token.value))
   560→        elif token.type == 'BOOL':
   561→            self.consume()
   562→            return Literal(token.value == 'TRUE')
   563→        elif token.type == 'ID':
   564→            name = self.consume().value
   565→            if self.match('LPAREN'):
   566→                return self.parse_function_call(name)
   567→            return Variable(name)
   568→        elif token.type == 'DOLLAR':
   569→            return self.parse_env_var()
   570→        elif token.type == 'LPAREN':
   571→            self.consume('LPAREN')
   572→            expr = self.parse_expression()
   573→            self.consume('RPAREN')
   574→            return expr
   575→        else:
   576→            raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected literal type {token.type}")
   577→
   578→    def parse_env_var(self) -> AST:
   579→        self.consume('DOLLAR')
   580→        if self.match('LBRACE'):
   581→            self.consume('LBRACE')
   582→            name = self.consume('ID').value
   583→            self.consume('RBRACE')
   584→            return Variable(f"${{{name}}}")
   585→        else:
   586→            name = self.consume('ID').value
   587→            return Variable(f"${name}")
   588→
   589→    def parse_function_call(self, name: str) -> AST:
   590→        self.consume('LPAREN')
   591→        args = []
   592→        kwargs = {}
   593→
   594→        while not self.match('RPAREN'):
   595→            arg = self.parse_expression()
   596→
   597→            if self.match('EQ'):
   598→                self.consume('EQ')
   599→                if isinstance(arg, Variable):
   600→                    # Check for dot notation (e.g., ttl.seconds=1)
   601→                    var_name = arg.name
   602→                    if '.' in var_name:
   603→                        parts = var_name.split('.')
   604→                        kwargs[var_name] = self.parse_expression()
   605→                    else:
   606→                        kwargs[arg.name] = self.parse_expression()
   607→                else:
   608→                    raise SyntaxError(f"Expected parameter name, got {arg}")
   609→            else:
   610→                args.append(('', arg))
   611→
   612→            if self.match('COMMA'):
   613→                self.consume('COMMA')
   614→            elif not self.match('RPAREN'):
   615→                raise SyntaxError("Expected ',' or ')' in function call")
   616→
   617→        self.consume('RPAREN')
   618→        call = FunctionCall(name)
   619→        for param_name, val in args:
   620→            call.args.append((param_name, val))
   621→        call.kwargs = kwargs
   622→        return call
   623→
   624→    def parse_expression(self) -> AST:
   625→        return self.parse_or_expr()
   626→
   627→    def parse_or_expr(self) -> AST:
   628→        left = self.parse_and_expr()
   629→        while self.match('OR'):
   630→            op = self.consume().value
   631→            right = self.parse_and_expr()
   632→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   633→        return left
   634→
   635→    def parse_and_expr(self) -> AST:
   636→        left = self.parse_equality()
   637→        while self.match('AND'):
   638→            op = self.consume().value
   639→            right = self.parse_equality()
   640→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   641→        return left
   642→
   643→    def parse_equality(self) -> AST:
   644→        left = self.parse_comparison()
   645→        while self.match('EQ_EXPR', '==') or self.match('NEQ', '!='):
   646→            op = self.consume().value
   647→            right = self.parse_comparison()
   648→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   649→        return left
   650→
   651→    def parse_comparison(self) -> AST:
   652→        left = self.parse_additive()
   653→        while self.match('LT', '<') or self.match('GT', '>') or self.match('LTE', '<=') or self.match('GTE', '>='):
   654→            op = self.consume().value
   655→            right = self.parse_additive()
   656→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   657→        return left
   658→
   659→    def parse_additive(self) -> AST:
   660→        left = self.parse_multiplicative()
   661→        while self.match('PERCENT'):
   662→            op = self.consume().value
   663→            right = self.parse_multiplicative()
   664→            left = StringConcat(left, right) if isinstance(left, (Variable, StringConcat)) else BinaryOp('%', left, right)
   665→        while self.match('PLUS') or self.match('MINUS'):
   666→            op = self.consume().value
   667→            right = self.parse_multiplicative()
   668→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   669→        return left
   670→
   671→    def parse_multiplicative(self) -> AST:
   672→        left = self.parse_unary()
   673→        while self.match('STAR') or self.match('SLASH'):
   674→            op = self.consume().value
   675→            right = self.parse_unary()
   676→            left = BinaryOp(op, left, right)
   677→        return left
   678→
   679→    def parse_unary(self) -> AST:
   680→        if self.match('NOT'):
   681→            op = self.consume().value
   682→            operand = self.parse_unary()
   683→            return UnaryOp(op, operand)
   684→        return self.parse_primary()
   685→
   686→    def parse_primary(self) -> AST:
   687→        token = self.current()
   688→        if token is None:
   689→            raise SyntaxError("Expected expression")
   690→
   691→        if token.type == 'STRING':
   692→            self.consume()
   693→            return Literal(escape_string(token.value[1:-1]))
   694→        elif token.type == 'NUMBER':
   695→            self.consume()
   696→            if '.' in token.value:
   697→                return Literal(float(token.value))
   698→            else:
   699→                return Literal(int(token.value))
   700→        elif token.type == 'BOOL':
   701→            self.consume()
   702→            return Literal(token.value == 'TRUE')
   703→        elif token.type == 'ID':
   704→            name = self.consume().value
   705→            if self.match('LPAREN'):
   706→                return self.parse_function_call(name)
   707→            if self.match('LBRACKET'):
   708→                # Array index access
   709→                self.consume('LBRACKET')
   710→                index = self.parse_expression()
   711→                self.consume('RBRACKET')
   712→                return IndexAccess(Variable(name), index)
   713→            return Variable(name)
   714→        elif token.type == 'DOLLAR':
   715→            return self.parse_env_var()
   716→        elif token.type == 'LPAREN':
   717→            self.consume('LPAREN')
   718→            expr = self.parse_expression()
   719→            self.consume('RPAREN')
   720→            return expr
   721→        else:
   722→            raise SyntaxError(f"Unexpected token in expression: {token}")
   723→
   724→    def parse_block(self) -> str:
   725→        """Parse a block of text (for run commands, success, requires)"""
   726→        self.consume('LBRACE')
   727→        lines = []
   728→        brace_depth = 1
   729→
   730→        while brace_depth > 0:
   731→            token = self.current()
   732→            if token is None:
   733→                raise SyntaxError("Unclosed block")
   734→
   735→            if token.type == 'NEWLINE':
   736→                lines.append('\n')
   737→                self.consume('NEWLINE')
   738→            elif token.type == 'LBRACE':
   739→                brace_depth += 1
   740→                lines.append('{')
   741→                self.consume('LBRACE')
   742→            elif token.type == 'RBRACE':
   743→                brace_depth -= 1
   744→                if brace_depth > 0:
   745→                    lines.append('}')
   746→                self.consume('RBRACE')
   747→            elif token.type == 'COMMENT':
   748→                self.consume('COMMENT')
   749→            else:
   750→                lines.append(token.value)
   751→                self.consume()
   752→
   753→        result = ''.join(lines).rstrip()
   754→        return result
   755→
   756→    def parse_block_text(self) -> str:
   757→        """Parse a block for success/requires (returns text for later parsing)"""
   758→        return self.parse_block()

   760→    def parse_cache_config(self) -> CacheConfig:
   761→        """Parse cache configuration block"""
   762→        self.consume('LBRACE')
   763→        config = CacheConfig()
   764→
   765→        while not self.match('RBRACE'):
   766→            if self.match('NEWLINE') or self.match('WHITESPACE'):
   767→                self.consume()
   768→                continue
   769→
   770→            if self.match('ID'):

   772→                key_name = key_token.value
   773→                self.consume()
   774→
   775→                if self.match('COLON'):
   776→                    self.consume('COLON')

   778→                    if key_name == 'enabled':
   779→                        if self.match('TRUE'):
   780→                            config.enabled = True
   781→                            self.consume('TRUE')
   782→                        elif self.match('FALSE'):

   784→                            self.consume('FALSE')
   785→                        else:
   786→                            val = self.parse_expression()
   787→                            if isinstance(val, Literal):
   788→                                config.enabled = val.value if isinstance(val.value, bool) else str(val.value).lower() == 'true'
   789→                    elif key_name == 'strategy':
   790→                        val = self.parse_expression()
   791→                        if isinstance(val, Literal):
   792→                            config.strategy = str(val.value)
   793→                    elif key_name == 'location':

   796→                            config.location = str(val.value)
   797→                    elif key_name == 'version':

   805→                    else:

   808→
   809→                if self.match('SEMICOLON'):

   811→            elif self.match('NEWLINE'):

   813→            elif self.match('WHITESPACE'):

   818→        self.consume('RBRACE')

   866→    def parse_key_config(self) -> CacheKey:
   867→        """Parse key configuration block"""
   868→        self.consume('LBRACE')
   869→        key = CacheKey()
   870→        include_list = []

   872→

   882→

   884→                    self.consume('COLON')

   887→                        self.consume('LBRACKET')
   888→
   889→                        while not self.match('RBRACKET'):

   899→
   900→                                if self.match('COMMA'):

   902→                            elif self.match('NEWLINE'):
   903→                                self.consume('NEWLINE')
   904→                            elif self.match('WHITESPACE'):
   905→                                self.consume('WHITESPACE')
   906→                        self.consume('RBRACKET')
   907→
   908→                    if self.match('SEMICOLON'):

   910→            elif self.match('NEWLINE'):
   911→                self.consume('NEWLINE')
   912→            elif self.match('WHITESPACE'):
   913→                self.consume('WHITESPACE')
   914→            else:
   915→                break
   916→
   917→        key.include = include_list if include_list else None
   918→        key.exclude = exclude_list if exclude_list else None
   919→        self.consume('RBRACE')

   922→    def parse_inputs_list(self) -> list[str]:

   944→    def parse_config(self, text: str) -> Config:
   945→        config = Config()
   946→        lines = text.split('\n')
   947→
   948→        for line in lines:
   949→            line = line.strip()
   950→            if not line or line.startswith('#'):
   951→                continue
   952→            if '=' in line:
   953→                key, value = line.split('=', 1)
   954→                key = key.strip()
   955→                value = value.strip()
   956→                if key == 'entry':
   957→                    config.entry = value.strip('"').strip("'")
   958→                elif key == 'clean_cwd':
   959→                    config.clean_cwd = value.lower() == 'true'
   960→                elif key == '[env]':
   961→                    pass
   962→                elif key.startswith('env.'):
   963→                    env_key = key[4:]
   964→                    config.env[env_key] = value.strip('"').strip("'")
   965→
   966→        # Parse TOML for env and other config
   967→        try:
   968→            # Find the TOML portion after the first [ or key=value
   969→            toml_start = text.find('\n\n')
   970→            if toml_start == -1:
   971→                toml_start = 0
   972→            toml_data = tomllib.loads(text[toml_start:])
   973→            if 'entry' in toml_data:
   974→                config.entry = toml_data['entry']
   975→            if 'clean_cwd' in toml_data:
   976→                config.clean_cwd = toml_data['clean_cwd']
   977→            if 'env' in toml_data:
   978→                config.env.update(toml_data['env'])
   979→            if 'cache' in toml_data:
   980→                cache_data = toml_data['cache']
   981→                if isinstance(cache_data, dict):
   982→                    if 'enabled' in cache_data:
   983→                        config.cache_enabled = cache_data['enabled']
   984→                    if 'location' in cache_data:
   985→                        config.cache_location = cache_data['location']
   986→                    if 'force_refresh' in cache_data:
   987→                        config.force_refresh = cache_data['force_refresh'] if isinstance(cache_data['force_refresh'], list) else [cache_data['force_refresh']]
   988→        except Exception:
   989→            pass  # Non-TOML format, skip
   990→
   991→        return config
   992→

   994→def tokenize_pipeline(text: str) -> list[Token]:
   995→    lexer = Lexer(text)
   996→    return lexer.tokenize()
   997→
   998→
   999→def parse_pipeline(text: str) -> list[Task]:
  1000→    tokens = tokenize_pipeline(text)
  1001→    parser = Parser(tokens)
  1002→    tasks = []
  1003→
  1004→    while parser.current() is not None:
  1005→        if parser.match('NEWLINE') or parser.match('WHITESPACE'):
  1006→            parser.consume()
  1007→            continue
  1008→        tasks.append(parser.parse_task())
  1009→
  1010→    return tasks

  1018→class CacheManager:
  1019→    """Manages cache storage and retrieval for tasks."""
  1020→
  1021→    def __init__(self, config: Config, workspace: str):
  1022→        self.config = config
  1023→        self.workspace = workspace
  1024→        # Determine cache location
  1025→        if config.cache_location:
  1026→            self.cache_dir = config.cache_location
  1027→        else:
  1028→            self.cache_dir = ".pipe-cache"
  1029→        self.cache_dir = os.path.abspath(self.cache_dir)
  1030→        os.makedirs(self.cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
  1031→
  1032→    def _get_cache_file(self, cache_key: str) -> str:
  1033→        """Get the cache file path for a given cache key."""
  1034→        # Use first 2 chars as subdirectory for organization
  1035→        safe_key = hashlib.sha256(cache_key.encode()).hexdigest()
  1036→        subdir = safe_key[:2]
  1037→        full_dir = os.path.join(self.cache_dir, subdir)
  1038→        os.makedirs(full_dir, exist_ok=True)
  1039→        return os.path.join(full_dir, safe_key)
  1040→
  1041→    def _hash_file(self, filepath: str) -> str:
  1042→        """Compute SHA256 hash of a file."""
  1043→        try:
  1044→            hasher = hashlib.sha256()
  1045→            with open(filepath, 'rb') as f:
  1046→                hasher.update(f.read())
  1047→            return hasher.hexdigest()
  1048→        except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
  1049→            return ""
  1050→
  1051→    def _hash_inputs(self, inputs: list[str], workspace: str) -> list[str]:
  1052→        """Compute hashes for all input files (supports globs)."""
  1053→        hashes = []
  1054→        for pattern in inputs:
  1055→            # Resolve workspace placeholder
  1056→            if '${workspace}' in pattern:
  1057→                pattern = pattern.replace('${workspace}', workspace)
  1058→            elif not os.path.isabs(pattern):
  1059→                pattern = os.path.join(workspace, pattern)
  1060→
  1061→            # Handle glob patterns
  1062→            matched_files = sorted(glob.glob(pattern))
  1063→            for filepath in matched_files:
  1064→                if os.path.isfile(filepath):
  1065→                    file_hash = self._hash_file(filepath)
  1066→                    if file_hash:
  1067→                        hashes.append(f"{filepath}:{file_hash}")
  1068→        return hashes
  1069→
  1070→    def _is_force_refresh(self, task_name: str) -> bool:
  1071→        """Check if task is in force_refresh list."""
  1072→        if not self.config.force_refresh:
  1073→            return False
  1074→        return task_name in self.config.force_refresh

  1076→    def generate_cache_key(
  1077→        self,
  1078→        task_name: str,
  1079→        params: dict[str, Any],
  1080→        cache_config: Optional[CacheConfig],
  1081→        inputs: Optional[list[str]],
  1082→        workspace: str
  1083→    ) -> Optional[str]:
  1084→        """Generate a cache key for a task execution."""
  1085→        # Check if caching is globally enabled
  1086→        if not self.config.cache_enabled:
  1087→            return None
  1088→
  1089→        # Check if task is force_refresh
  1090→        if self._is_force_refresh(task_name):
  1091→            return None
  1092→
  1093→        # Check if task has cache config and it's enabled
  1094→        if cache_config is None or not cache_config.enabled:
  1095→            return None
  1096→
  1097→        # Build key components
  1098→        key_parts = []
  1099→
  1100→        # Task name
  1101→        key_parts.append(f"task:{task_name}")
  1102→
  1103→        # Version if specified
  1104→        if cache_config.version:
  1105→            key_parts.append(f"ver:{cache_config.version}")
  1106→
  1107→        # Inputs hash
  1108→        if inputs:
  1109→            input_hashes = self._hash_inputs(inputs, workspace)
  1110→            # Sort for consistent ordering
  1111→            input_hashes.sort()
  1112→            for h in input_hashes:
  1113→                key_parts.append(f"in:{h}")
  1114→
  1115→        # Parameters based on include/exclude rules
  1116→        param_keys = list(params.keys())
  1117→
  1118→        if cache_config.key:
  1119→            if cache_config.key.include is not None:
  1120→                # Only include specified params
  1121→                param_keys = [k for k in param_keys if k in cache_config.key.include]
  1122→            if cache_config.key.exclude is not None:
  1123→                # Exclude specified params
  1124→                param_keys = [k for k in param_keys if k not in cache_config.key.exclude]
  1125→
  1126→        # Sort for consistency
  1127→        param_keys.sort()
  1128→        for pk in param_keys:
  1129→            key_parts.append(f"p:{pk}:{params[pk]}")
  1130→
  1131→        # Hash the entire key
  1132→        key_string = "|".join(key_parts)
  1133→        return hashlib.sha256(key_string.encode()).hexdigest()
  1134→
  1135→    def check_cache(
  1136→        self,
  1137→        cache_key: str,
  1138→        cache_config: CacheConfig,
  1139→        job: Job
  1140→    ) -> Optional[CacheEntry]:
  1141→        """Check if cache exists and is valid for the given key."""
  1142→        if not cache_key:
  1143→            return None
  1144→
  1145→        cache_file = self._get_cache_file(cache_key)
  1146→        cache_meta_file = cache_file + ".json"
  1147→
  1148→        # Check if cache exists
  1149→        if not os.path.exists(cache_meta_file):
  1150→            return None
  1151→
  1152→        # Load cache metadata
  1153→        try:
  1154→            with open(cache_meta_file, 'r') as f:
  1155→                cache_data = json.load(f)
  1156→        except (json.JSONDecodeError, IOError):
  1157→            return None
  1158→
  1159→        # Check TTL if applicable
  1160→        if cache_config.ttl:
  1161→            created_at = cache_data.get('created_at', 0)
  1162→            age = time.time() - created_at
  1163→
  1164→            ttl_seconds = 0
  1165→            if cache_config.ttl.seconds is not None:
  1166→                ttl_seconds += cache_config.ttl.seconds
  1167→            if cache_config.ttl.minutes is not None:
  1168→                ttl_seconds += cache_config.ttl.minutes * 60
  1169→            if cache_config.ttl.hours is not None:
  1170→                ttl_seconds += cache_config.ttl.hours * 3600
  1171→            if cache_config.ttl.days is not None:
  1172→                ttl_seconds += cache_config.ttl.days * 86400
  1173→
  1174→            if ttl_seconds > 0 and age > ttl_seconds:
  1175→                # Cache is stale, delete it
  1176→                try:
  1177→                    os.remove(cache_meta_file)
  1178→                    output_dir = cache_data.get('output_dir')
  1179→                    if output_dir and os.path.exists(output_dir):
  1180→                        shutil.rmtree(output_dir, ignore_errors=True)
  1181→                except OSError:
  1182→                    pass
  1183→                return None
  1184→
  1185→        # Validate content if strategy is 'content'
  1186→        if cache_config.strategy == 'content':
  1187→            # Verify all input files still match their hashes
  1188→            for in_hash in cache_data.get('input_hashes', []):
  1189→                if ':' in in_hash:
  1190→                    filepath, expected_hash = in_hash.rsplit(':', 1)
  1191→                    actual_hash = self._hash_file(filepath)
  1192→                    if actual_hash != expected_hash:
  1193→                        return None
  1194→
  1195→        # Reconstruct CacheEntry
  1196→        entry = CacheEntry(
  1197→            cache_key=cache_data.get('cache_key', ''),
  1198→            task_name=cache_data.get('task_name', ''),
  1199→            params=cache_data.get('params', {}),
  1200→            stdout=cache_data.get('stdout', ''),
  1201→            stderr=cache_data.get('stderr', ''),
  1202→            exit_code=cache_data.get('exit_code', 0),
  1203→            output_dir=cache_data.get('output_dir'),
  1204→            created_at=cache_data.get('created_at', time.time()),
  1205→            input_hashes=cache_data.get('input_hashes', []),
  1206→            success=cache_data.get('success', {})
  1207→        )
  1208→
  1209→        return entry

  1211→    def store_cache(

  1215→        params: dict[str, Any],

  1221→        workspace: str,

  1224→        """Store a cache entry."""
  1225→        if not cache_key:

  1227→
  1228→        cache_file = self._get_cache_file(cache_key)

  1230→

  1247→
  1248→        # Write cache metadata

  1256→# Evaluate expressions
  1257→class EvaluationContext:
  1258→    def __init__(self, workspace: str, params: dict[str, Any], output_dir: str):
  1259→        self.workspace = workspace
  1260→        self.params = params
  1261→        self.output_dir = output_dir
  1262→        self.variables: dict[str, Any] = {}
  1263→        self.functions = self._init_functions()
  1264→
  1265→    def _init_functions(self):
  1266→        return {
  1267→            'exists': self.exists_func,
  1268→            'contains': self.contains_func,
  1269→            'len': self.len_func,
  1270→            'equals': self.equals_func,
  1271→            'fail': self.fail_func,
  1272→            'fails': self.fails_func,
  1273→        }
  1274→
  1275→    def exists_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> bool:
  1276→        if len(args) != 1:
  1277→            raise ValueError("exists() takes exactly 1 argument")
  1278→        filepath = self.evaluate_expr(args[0])
  1279→        return os.path.exists(filepath) if isinstance(filepath, str) else False
  1280→
  1281→    def contains_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> bool:
  1282→        if len(args) < 2:
  1283→            raise ValueError("contains() takes at least 2 arguments")
  1284→        target = self.evaluate_expr(args[0])
  1285→        pattern = self.evaluate_expr(args[1])
  1286→        if not isinstance(target, str) or not isinstance(pattern, str):
  1287→            return False
  1288→        return pattern in target
  1289→
  1290→    def len_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> int:
  1291→        if len(args) != 1:
  1292→            raise ValueError("len() takes exactly 1 argument")
  1293→        arr = self.evaluate_expr(args[0])
  1294→        return len(arr) if isinstance(arr, list) else 0
  1295→
  1296→    def equals_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> bool:
  1297→        if len(args) != 2:
  1298→            raise ValueError("equals() takes exactly 2 arguments")
  1299→        left = self.evaluate_expr(args[0])
  1300→        right = self.evaluate_expr(args[1])
  1301→        return left == right
  1302→
  1303→    def fail_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> bool:
  1304→        return False
  1305→
  1306→    def fails_func(self, args: list[AST]) -> bool:
  1307→        return True
  1308→
  1309→    def evaluate_expr(self, node: AST) -> Any:
  1310→        if isinstance(node, Literal):
  1311→            return node.value
  1312→        elif isinstance(node, Variable):
  1313→            name = node.name
  1314→            if name.startswith('${') or name.startswith('$'):
  1315→                return os.environ.get(name[2:] if name.startswith('${') else name[1:], '')
  1316→            if name == 'stdout' or name == 'stderr':
  1317→                return self.variables.get(name, '')
  1318→            if name == 'exit_code':
  1319→                return self.variables.get('exit_code', 0)
  1320→            if name in self.variables:
  1321→                return self.variables[name]
  1322→            return self.params.get(name, '')
  1323→        elif isinstance(node, BinaryOp):
  1324→            left = self.evaluate_expr(node.left)
  1325→            right = self.evaluate_expr(node.right)
  1326→            if node.op == '+':
  1327→                return left + right
  1328→            elif node.op == '-':
  1329→                return left - right
  1330→            elif node.op == '*':
  1331→                return left * right
  1332→            elif node.op == '/':
  1333→                return left / right
  1334→            elif node.op == '%':
  1335→                return str(left) + str(right)
  1336→            elif node.op == '==':
  1337→                return left == right
  1338→            elif node.op == '!=':
  1339→                return left != right
  1340→            elif node.op == '<':
  1341→                return left < right
  1342→            elif node.op == '>':
  1343→                return left > right
  1344→            elif node.op == '<=':
  1345→                return left <= right
  1346→            elif node.op == '>=':
  1347→                return left >= right
  1348→            elif node.op == '&&':
  1349→                return left and right
  1350→            elif node.op == '||':
  1351→                return left or right
  1352→            elif node.op == '!':
  1353→                return not right
  1354→        elif isinstance(node, UnaryOp):
  1355→            operand = self.evaluate_expr(node.operand)
  1356→            if node.op == '!':
  1357→                return not operand
  1358→        elif isinstance(node, IndexAccess):
  1359→            arr = self.evaluate_expr(node.arr)
  1360→            idx = self.evaluate_expr(node.index)
  1361→            if isinstance(arr, list) and isinstance(idx, int):
  1362→                return arr[idx]
  1363→        elif isinstance(node, StringConcat):
  1364→            left = str(self.evaluate_expr(node.left))
  1365→            right = str(self.evaluate_expr(node.right))
  1366→            return left + right
  1367→        return None

  1370→def parse_success_block(text: str, context: EvaluationContext) -> dict[str, bool]:
  1371→    """Parse a success block and evaluate it"""
  1372→    # Create a simple parser for the success block syntax
  1373→    results = {}
  1374→    lines = text.strip().split('\n')
  1375→    current_name = None
  1376→    current_body = []
  1377→
  1378→    in_block = False
  1379→    brace_depth = 0
  1380→
  1381→    for line in lines:
  1382→        stripped = line.strip()
  1383→        if not stripped:
  1384→            continue
  1385→
  1386→        # Check if this is a new criterion name
  1387→        if ':' in stripped and not in_block:
  1388→            if current_name:
  1389→                # Evaluate previous criterion
  1390→                results[current_name] = evaluate_success_body('\n'.join(current_body), context)
  1391→            parts = stripped.split(':')
  1392→            current_name = parts[0].strip()
  1393→            current_body = []
  1394→            if len(parts) > 1:
  1395→                current_body.append(parts[1].strip())
  1396→                brace_depth = 0
  1397→                if '{' in stripped:
  1398→                    brace_depth = 1
  1399→        elif in_block:
  1400→            current_body.append(stripped)
  1401→            brace_depth += stripped.count('{') - stripped.count('}')
  1402→            if brace_depth <= 0 and current_name:
  1403→                results[current_name] = evaluate_success_body('\n'.join(current_body), context)
  1404→                current_name = None
  1405→                current_body = []
  1406→                in_block = False
  1407→
  1408→    # Handle last criterion
  1409→    if current_name and current_body:
  1410→        results[current_name] = evaluate_success_body('\n'.join(current_body), context)
  1411→
  1412→    return results

  1419→        # This is a simplified evaluation
  1420→        # In a full implementation, we'd parse and evaluate the control flow
  1421→        return True  # Simplified - return true for now
  1422→    return True

  1425→def execute_pipeline(

  1438→    # Build task lookup

  1441→    # Determine entry task

  1443→    # Parse entry name and params
  1444→    if '(' in entry_name:

  1446→        # Simplified - just use the task name
  1447→        entry_task_name = task_map.get(entry_task_name, Task(name=entry_task_name))
  1448→    else:

  1450→
  1451→    entry_task = task_map.get(entry_task_name)

  1471→    # Execute the task

  1483→    return success

  1486→def execute_task(
  1487→    task: Task,
  1488→    params: dict[str, Any],
  1489→    workspace: str,
  1490→    output_dir: str,
  1491→    job: Job,
  1492→    job_counter: list[int],
  1493→    jobs: list[Job],
  1494→    task_map: dict[str, Task],
  1495→    config: Config,
  1496→) -> bool:
  1497→    """Execute a single task. Returns True if successful."""
  1498→    print(json.dumps({"event": "TASK_RUNNING", "job_id": job.job_id}))
  1499→
  1500→    # Store inputs in job
  1501→    job.inputs = task.inputs
  1502→
  1503→    # Store cache config in job (as dict for JSON serialization)
  1504→    if task.cache:
  1505→        job.cache = {
  1506→            "enabled": task.cache.enabled,
  1507→            "strategy": task.cache.strategy,
  1508→            "location": task.cache.location,
  1509→            "version": task.cache.version,
  1510→        }
  1511→        if task.cache.ttl:
  1512→            job.cache["ttl"] = {
  1513→                "seconds": task.cache.ttl.seconds,
  1514→                "minutes": task.cache.ttl.minutes,
  1515→                "hours": task.cache.ttl.hours,
  1516→                "days": task.cache.ttl.days,
  1517→            }
  1518→    else:
  1519→        job.cache = None
  1520→
  1521→    # Initialize cache manager
  1522→    cache_manager = CacheManager(config, workspace)
  1523→
  1524→    # Generate cache key
  1525→    cache_key = cache_manager.generate_cache_key(
  1526→        task.name,
  1527→        params,
  1528→        task.cache,
  1529→        task.inputs,
  1530→        workspace
  1531→    )
  1532→    job.cache_key = cache_key
  1533→
  1534→    # Check cache if key exists
  1535→    cache_entry = None
  1536→    if cache_key:
  1537→        print(json.dumps({"event": "CACHE_CHECK", "job_id": job.job_id, "cache_key": cache_key[:16] + "..."}))
  1538→        cache_entry = cache_manager.check_cache(cache_key, task.cache, job)
  1539→        if cache_entry:
  1540→            print(json.dumps({"event": "CACHE_HIT", "job_id": job.job_id, "cache_key": cache_key[:16] + "..."}))
  1541→            job.cache_hit = True
  1542→            # Restore from cache
  1543→            job.stdout = cache_entry.stdout
  1544→            job.stderr = cache_entry.stderr
  1545→            job.exit_code = cache_entry.exit_code
  1546→            job.success = cache_entry.success
  1547→            job.duration = 0.0
  1548→
  1549→            # Restore output files if output_dir was cached
  1550→            if cache_entry.output_dir:
  1551→                # Copy cached output to the expected output location
  1552→                if task.output:
  1553→                    output_str = resolve_template(task.output, params, workspace)
  1554→                    if '${workspace}' in output_str:
  1555→                        target_output_dir = output_str.replace('${workspace}', workspace)
  1556→                    else:
  1557→                        target_output_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, output_str)
  1558→                    # Remove existing target and copy cached
  1559→                    if os.path.exists(target_output_dir):
  1560→                        shutil.rmtree(target_output_dir)
  1561→                    shutil.copytree(cache_entry.output_dir, target_output_dir)
  1562→                    job.output = target_output_dir
  1563→
  1564→            return all(v for v in cache_entry.success.values() if isinstance(v, bool))
  1565→        else:
  1566→            print(json.dumps({"event": "CACHE_MISS", "job_id": job.job_id, "cache_key": cache_key[:16] + "..."}))
  1567→
  1568→    # Resolve task parameters
  1569→    # Merge default params with provided ones
  1570→    resolved_params = {}
  1571→    for p in task.params:
  1572→        if p.name in params:
  1573→            resolved_params[p.name] = params[p.name]
  1574→        elif p.has_default:
  1575→            resolved_params[p.name] = p.default
  1576→        else:
  1577→            raise InvalidPipelineError(f"Missing required parameter '{p.name}' for task '{task.name}'")
  1578→
  1579→    job.params = resolved_params
  1580→
  1581→    # Resolve output directory
  1582→    output_path = None
  1583→    if task.output:
  1584→        output_str = resolve_template(task.output, resolved_params, workspace)
  1585→        if '${workspace}' in output_str:
  1586→            output_path = output_str.replace('${workspace}', workspace)
  1587→        else:
  1588→            output_path = os.path.join(output_dir, output_str)
  1589→        os.makedirs(output_path, exist_ok=True)
  1590→        job.output = output_path
  1591→
  1592→    # Setup context for evaluation
  1593→    context = EvaluationContext(workspace, resolved_params, output_dir or output_path)
  1594→
  1595→    # Execute requires first
  1596→    if task.requires:
  1597→        requires_success = execute_requires(task.requires, resolved_params, workspace, output_dir,
  1598→            job, job_counter, jobs, task_map, config)
  1599→        if not requires_success:
  1600→            job.success = {"requires": False}
  1601→            job.exit_code = 1
  1602→            return False
  1603→
  1604→    # Execute run commands
  1605→    if task.run:
  1606→        # Setup working directory
  1607→        work_dir = workspace
  1608→        if config.clean_cwd:
  1609→            # Clean the working directory
  1610→            for f in os.listdir(work_dir):
  1611→                path = os.path.join(work_dir, f)
  1612→                if os.path.isfile(path):
  1613→                    os.remove(path)
  1614→
  1615→        # Execute commands
  1616→        stdout_lines = []
  1617→        stderr_lines = []
  1618→        exit_code = 0
  1619→        timed_out = False
  1620→
  1621→        # Parse and execute each line
  1622→        run_lines = task.run.strip().split('\n')
  1623→        for line in run_lines:
  1624→            line = resolve_template(line, resolved_params, workspace)
  1625→            if not line.strip():
  1626→                continue
  1627→
  1628→            try:
  1629→                start_time = time.time()
  1630→                proc = subprocess.Popen(
  1631→                    line,
  1632→                    shell=True,
  1633→                    cwd=work_dir,
  1634→                    stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
  1635→                    stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
  1636→                    text=True,
  1637→                )
  1638→
  1639→                # Handle timeout
  1640→                timeout = task.timeout
  1641→                if timeout:
  1642→                    try:
  1643→                        stdout, stderr = proc.communicate(timeout=timeout)
  1644→                        stdout_lines.append(stdout)
  1645→                        stderr_lines.append(stderr)
  1646→                    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
  1647→                        proc.terminate()
  1648→                        try:
  1649→                            proc.wait(timeout=5)
  1650→                        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
  1651→                            proc.kill()
  1652→                        timed_out = True
  1653→                        exit_code = 124
  1654→                        stdout_lines.append(proc.stdout.read() if proc.stdout else '')
  1655→                        stderr_lines.append(proc.stderr.read() if proc.stderr else 'Command timed out')
  1656→                else:
  1657→                    stdout, stderr = proc.communicate()
  1658→                    stdout_lines.append(stdout)
  1659→                    stderr_lines.append(stderr)
  1660→
  1661→                exit_code = proc.returncode if not timed_out else 124
  1662→                end_time = time.time()
  1663→                job.duration = end_time - start_time
  1664→            except Exception as e:
  1665→                stderr_lines.append(str(e))
  1666→                exit_code = 1
  1667→                timed_out = False
  1668→
  1669→        # Cleanup stdout/stderr
  1670→        stdout = ''.join(stdout_lines).replace('\u2018', "'").replace('\u2019', "'")
  1671→        stderr = ''.join(stderr_lines).replace('\u2018', "'").replace('\u2019', "'")
  1672→
  1673→        job.stdout = stdout
  1674→        job.stderr = stderr
  1675→        job.exit_code = exit_code
  1676→        job.timed_out = timed_out
  1677→
  1678→        # Check exit code as success criteria
  1679→        success_results = {'exit_code': exit_code == 0}
  1680→
  1681→        # Evaluate success block if present
  1682→        if task.success:
  1683→            try:
  1684→                context.variables['stdout'] = stdout
  1685→                context.variables['stderr'] = stderr
  1686→                context.variables['exit_code'] = exit_code
  1687→                success_results.update(parse_success_block(task.success, context))
  1688→            except Exception as e:
  1689→                success_results['error'] = str(e)
  1690→
  1691→        job.success = success_results
  1692→
  1693→        # Overall success is all criteria passing
  1694→        overall_success = all(v for v in success_results.values())
  1695→    else:
  1696→        overall_success = True
  1697→        job.success = {}
  1698→        job.exit_code = 0
  1699→
  1700→    # Store cache if cache_key exists and task was successful
  1701→    if cache_key and overall_success:
  1702→        cache_manager.store_cache(
  1703→            cache_key=cache_key,
  1704→            task_name=task.name,
  1705→            params=resolved_params,
  1706→            stdout=job.stdout,
  1707→            stderr=job.stderr,
  1708→            exit_code=job.exit_code,
  1709→            output_dir=output_path,  # Store the output directory path
  1710→            inputs=task.inputs,
  1711→            workspace=workspace,
  1712→            success=job.success
  1713→        )
  1714→
  1715→    return overall_success

  1718→def execute_requires(
  1719→    requires_text: str,
  1720→    params: dict[str, Any],
  1721→    workspace: str,
  1722→    output_dir: str,
  1723→    parent_job: Job,
  1724→    job_counter: list[int],
  1725→    jobs: list[Job],
  1726→    task_map: dict[str, Task],
  1727→    config: Config,
  1728→) -> bool:
  1729→    """Execute the requires block. Returns True if all required tasks succeed."""
  1730→    # Parse the requires block using the lexer and parser
  1731→    try:
  1732→        tokens = tokenize_pipeline(requires_text)
  1733→    except SyntaxError as e:
  1734→        print(f"SYNTAX_ERROR: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1735→        return False
  1736→
  1737→    parser = Parser(tokens)
  1738→
  1739→    while parser.current() is not None:
  1740→        # Skip whitespace/newline
  1741→        if parser.match('NEWLINE') or parser.match('WHITESPACE'):
  1742→            parser.consume()
  1743→            continue
  1744→
  1745→        # Check for fails() call
  1746→        if parser.match('ID') and parser.current().value == 'fails':
  1747→            fails_token = parser.consume()  # consume 'fails'
  1748→            if parser.match('LPAREN'):
  1749→                parser.consume('LPAREN')
  1750→                if parser.match('ID'):
  1751→                    task_name = parser.consume().value
  1752→                    task = task_map.get(task_name)
  1753→                    if task:
  1754→                        parsed_params = {}
  1755→                        if parser.match('LPAREN'):
  1756→                            parser.consume('LPAREN')
  1757→                            while not parser.match('RPAREN'):
  1758→                                if parser.match('NEWLINE') or parser.match('WHITESPACE'):
  1759→                                    parser.consume()
  1760→                                    continue
  1761→                                param_expr = parser.parse_expression()
  1762→                                if isinstance(param_expr, Variable):
  1763→                                    parsed_params[param_expr.name] = None
  1764→                                if parser.match('COMMA'):
  1765→                                    parser.consume('COMMA')
  1766→                            parser.consume('RPAREN')
  1767→                        job_id = job_counter[0]
  1768→                        job_counter[0] += 1
  1769→                        job = Job(job_id=job_id, task=task_name, params={}, parent=parent_job.job_id)
  1770→                        jobs.append(job)
  1771→                        print(json.dumps({"event": "TASK_STARTED", "job_id": job_id}))
  1772→                        execute_task(task, parsed_params, workspace, output_dir, job, job_counter, jobs, task_map, config)

  2039→def evaluate_expr_for_cache(expr: AST) -> Any:
  2040→    """Evaluate an expression that appears as a value in CachedTask kwargs."""

  2043→    elif isinstance(expr, Variable):

  2045→        if name.startswith('${') or name.startswith('$'):

  2047→            return os.environ.get(name[2:] if name.startswith('${') else name[1:], '')
  2048→        return None
  2049→    elif isinstance(expr, dict):
  2050→        return {k: evaluate_expr_for_cache(v) for k, v in expr.items()}
  2051→    elif isinstance(expr, list):
  2052→        return [evaluate_expr_for_cache(item) for item in expr]

  2063→    result = template

  2067→    def replace_param(match):
